description: The package spatialGE provides a collection of tools for the visualization of gene expression from spatially-resolved transcriptomic experiments. The data input methods have been designed so that any data can be analyzed as long as it contains gene expression counts per region of interest (ROI), spot, or cell, and the spatial coordinates of those ROIs, spots, or cells, as it is generated in platforms such as GeoMx, Visium, and CosMx-SMI. The spatialge.Preprocessing module performs initial data ingestion, filtering, transform and pseudo-bulk operations to prepare data for further processing. Order of operations are\: ingestion, filter, pseudobulk, transform.
author: Edwin Huang, Ted Liefeld, Thorin Tabor, Michael Reich;UCSD - Mesirov Lab
plan:
## spatialGE.Preprocessing GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
spatialGE.Preprocessing is a GenePattern module that The package spatialGE provides a collection of tools for the visualization of gene expression from spatially-resolved transcriptomic experiments. The data input methods have been designed so that any data can be analyzed as long as it contains gene expression counts per region of interest (ROI), spot, or cell, and the spatial coordinates of those ROIs, spots, or cells, as it is generated in platforms such as GeoMx, Visium, and CosMx-SMI. The spatialge.Preprocessing module performs initial data ingestion, filtering, transform and pseudo-bulk operations to prepare data for further processing. Order of operations are\: ingestion, filter, pseudobulk, transform.

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 32 parameters:
- Required: input.data.archive, verbose, transform.data, output.filename, pseudobulk, pseudobulk.plot.meta, pseudobulk.heatmap.num.displayed.genes, distribution.plot.meta, input.clinical.data, filter.data
- Optional: pseudobulk.max.var.genes, spot.min.reads, spot.min.genes, spot.max.reads, spot.max.genes, transform.scale.f, transform.num.regression.genes, transform.min.spots.or.cells, spot.min.percent, spot.max.percent, gene.min.reads, gene.max.reads, gene.min.spots, gene.max.spots, gene.min.percent, gene.max.percent, filter.samples, rm.tissue, rm.spots, rm.genes, rm.genes.regex, spot.percentage.genes.regex

### 3. Command Line
```
Rscript /spatialGE/spatialge_preprocessing_wrapper.R <input.data.archive> <input.clinical.data> <verbose> <transform.data> <output.filename> <pseudobulk> <pseudobulk.max.var.genes> <pseudobulk.plot.meta> <pseudobulk.heatmap.num.displayed.genes> <distribution.plot.meta> <spot.min.reads> <spot.min.genes> <spot.max.reads> <spot.max.genes> <transform.scale.f> <transform.num.regression.genes> <transform.min.spots.or.cells> <spot.min.percent> <spot.max.percent> <gene.min.reads> <gene.max.reads> <gene.min.spots> <gene.max.spots> <gene.min.percent> <gene.max.percent> <filter.samples> <rm.tissue> <rm.spots> <rm.genes> <rm.genes.regex> <spot.percentage.genes.regex> <filter.data>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/spatialgp.preprocessing\:0.9

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: input.data.archive
parameters.description: Input data archive in gz format. Should match directory structure as defined by spatialGE as described here.

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Output additional files including summarized STList, distribution_plots before and after filtering, and pseudobulk PCA plot and heatmap.

parameters.name: transform.data
parameters.description: None, log or sct. If log, log-normalization is performed. If sct, then the SCTransform method is applied. SCT is a variance stabilizing transformation to UMI count data using a regularized Negative Binomial regression model.

parameters.name: output.filename
parameters.description: The basename to use for output file (no need to add ".txt" at the end)

parameters.name: pseudobulk
parameters.description: Perform pseudobulk to creates (pseudo) bulk RNAseq data sets by combining all counts from each sample. Then log transform the pseudo bulk and perform PCA. Note that the spatial coordinate information is not considered here, which is intended only as an exploratory analysis analysis.

parameters.name: pseudobulk.max.var.genes
parameters.description: The number of most variable genes (standard deviation) to use in pseudobulk analysis.

parameters.name: pseudobulk.plot.meta
parameters.description: A string indicating the name of the variable in the sample metadata to color points in the PCA plot.

parameters.name: pseudobulk.heatmap.num.displayed.genes
parameters.description: The number of genes to display in the pseudobulk heatmap, selected based on decreasing order of standard deviation across samples.

parameters.name: distribution.plot.meta
parameters.description: Vector of variables in x@spatial_meta to plot distributions. If 'total_counts', the function plots the counts per spot/cell. If 'total_genes', the function plots the number of genes per spot/cell are plotted.

parameters.name: spot.min.reads
parameters.description: The minimum number of total reads for a spot to be retained

parameters.name: spot.min.genes
parameters.description: the minimum number of non-zero counts for a spot to be retained

parameters.name: spot.max.reads
parameters.description: The maximum number of total reads for a spot to be retained

parameters.name: spot.max.genes
parameters.description: The maximum number of non-zero counts for a spot to be retained.

parameters.name: transform.scale.f
parameters.description: The scale factor used in logarithmic transformation.

parameters.name: transform.num.regression.genes
parameters.description: The number of genes to be used in the regression model during SCTransform. The function sctransform\:\:vst makes a random gene selection based on this number.

parameters.name: transform.min.spots.or.cells
parameters.description: The minimum number of spots/cells to be used in the regression model fit by sctransform\:\:vst.

parameters.name: spot.min.percent
parameters.description: The minimum percentage of counts for features defined by spot_pct_expr for a spot to be retained.

parameters.name: spot.max.percent
parameters.description: The maximum percentage of counts for features defined by spot_pct_expr for a spot to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.min.reads
parameters.description: The minimum number of total reads for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.max.reads
parameters.description: The maximum number of total reads for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.min.spots
parameters.description: The minimum number of spots with non-zero counts for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.max.spots
parameters.description: The maximum number of spots with non-zero counts for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.min.percent
parameters.description: The minimum percentage of spots with non-zero counts for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: gene.max.percent
parameters.description: The maximum percentage of spots with non-zero counts for a gene to be retained.

parameters.name: filter.samples
parameters.description: Samples (as in names(x@counts)) to perform filtering.

parameters.name: rm.tissue
parameters.description: Sample (as in names(x@counts)) to remove from STlist. Removes samples in x@counts, x@tr_counts, x@spatial_meta, x@gene_meta, and x@sample_meta.

parameters.name: rm.spots
parameters.description: Vector of spot/cell IDs to remove. Removes spots/cells in x@counts, x@tr_counts, and x@spatial_meta.

parameters.name: rm.genes
parameters.description: Vector of gene names to remove from STlist. Removes genes in x@counts, x@tr_counts, and x@gene_meta

parameters.name: rm.genes.regex
parameters.description: A regular expression that matches genes to remove. Removes genes in x@counts, x@tr_counts, and x@gene_meta.

parameters.name: spot.percentage.genes.regex
parameters.description: a expression to use with spot_minpct and spot_maxpct. By default '^MT-'.

parameters.name: input.clinical.data
parameters.description: Metadata associated with each sample in a csv file. The sample names are in the first column, and they must match the names of the folders containing the data.

parameters.name: filter.data
parameters.description: Filter data, T/F
